content:
# Acts Chapter 20 -- Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Chapter:** Acts 20
**Source Manuscript:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), PDF pages 250-251

---

## Summary Statistics
- **Total verses:** 38
- **Verses with disagreements:** 37 (only v.5 had near-perfect agreement across all three, differing only in preposition "in/at")
- **Total individual disagreements logged:** 72
- **Categories:** Word choice, structural/syntactic, interpretive expansion, theological/textual

---

## Full Disagreement Table

| Verse | Element | EMET (Literal) | LOGOS (Formal) | PNEUMA (Dynamic) | SOPHIA Decision | Category |
|-------|---------|----------------|----------------|-------------------|-----------------|----------|
| 1 | Verb tense | "the uproar ceased" | "the uproar had ceased" | "the uproar finally died down" | "the uproar had ceased" | Structural |
| 1 | Departure verbs | "having embraced [them], departed to journey into Macedonia" | "took leave of them and departed to go into Macedonia" | "embraced them with words of encouragement, said his goodbyes, and set out for Macedonia" | "embraced them and departed to go into Macedonia" | Word choice / interpretive |
| 2 | "parts" vs "regions" | "those parts" | "those regions" | "those regions" | "those regions" | Word choice |
| 2 | Speech description | "with much speech" | "with many words" | "pouring out encouragement everywhere he went" | "with many words" | Word choice / interpretive expansion |
| 2 | Arrival phrasing | "he came into Greece" | "he came into Greece" | "eventually arrived in Greece" | "he came into Greece" | Interpretive expansion |
| 3 | Plot construction | "a plot having been made against him" | "when a plot was formed against him" | "he discovered a Jewish plot against him" | "when a plot was formed against him" | Structural |
| 3 | Decision phrasing | "[a] decision came-about to return" | "he determined to return" | "he decided to go back" | "he decided to return" | Word choice |
| 4 | Sopater description order | "Sopater [son] of Pyrrhus, a Berean" | "Sopater of Berea, the son of Pyrrhus" | "Sopater son of Pyrrhus from Berea" | "Sopater son of Pyrrhus, a Berean" | Structural |
| 4 | Asian companions | "of [the] Asians" | "of Asia" | "from the province of Asia" | "of Asia" | Word choice / interpretive |
| 5 | Preposition | "in Troas" | "at Troas" | "in Troas" | "at Troas" | Word choice |
| 6 | Sailing verb | "sailed-out from Philippi" | "sailed from Philippi" | "sailed from Philippi" | "sailed from Philippi" | Word choice |
| 6 | Festival name | "the days of Unleavened-Bread" | "the days of Unleavened Bread" | "the Festival of Unleavened Bread" | "the days of Unleavened Bread" | Interpretive expansion |
| 6 | Arrival at Troas | "came to them in Troas" | "came to them at Troas" | "caught up with them in Troas" | "came to them at Troas" | Word choice / interpretive |
| 6 | Duration | "seven days" | "seven days" | "a week" | "seven days" | Word choice |
| 7 | Gathering construction | "we having been gathered-together" | "when we were gathered together" | "when we had gathered" | "when we had gathered together" | Structural |
| 7 | Paul's speaking | "Paul was discoursing to them" | "Paul was discoursing with them" | "Paul spoke to them" | "Paul was speaking to them" | Word choice |
| 7 | Prolonged speech | "he was extending the speech" | "he prolonged his speech" | "he kept talking" | "he prolonged his speech" | Word choice |
| 8 | Lamp quantity | "sufficient lamps" | "many lamps" | "plenty of lamps burning" | "many lamps" | Word choice / interpretive |
| 8 | Room description | "the upper-room where we were gathered-together" | "the upper room where we were gathered together" | "the upstairs room where we were meeting" | "the upper room where we were gathered together" | Word choice |
| 9 | Window position | "sitting upon the window" | "sitting in the window" | "sitting in the window" | "sitting in the window" | Word choice |
| 9 | Sleep description | "being borne-down by a deep sleep" | "was being overcome by deep sleep" | "sank deeper and deeper into sleep" | "was being overcome by deep sleep" | Word choice / interpretive |
| 9 | Paul's continuing speech | "while Paul discoursed yet-longer" | "as Paul continued speaking at length" | "As Paul talked on and on" | "as Paul continued speaking at length" | Word choice |
| 9 | Taken up | "was taken-up dead" | "was taken up dead" | "was picked up dead" | "was taken up dead" | Word choice |
| 10 | Paul's descent and action | "having gone-down, fell-upon him and having embraced [him]" | "went down and fell upon him, and having embraced him" | "went down, threw himself on the young man, and wrapped his arms around him" | "went down and fell upon him, and having embraced him" | Structural / interpretive |
| 10 | Command to crowd | "Do not be troubled" | "Do not be troubled" | "Stop panicking!" | "Do not be troubled" | Word choice / interpretive |
| 10 | Life declaration | "for his life is in him" | "for his life is in him" | "He's alive!" | "for his life is in him" | Interpretive expansion |
| 11 | Eating verb | "having tasted" | "eaten" | "ate" | "eaten" | Word choice |
| 11 | Departure sentence | "having conversed for a considerable-time until dawn, thus he departed" | "having conversed at length until dawn, so he departed" | "He went on talking until dawn broke, and then he left" | "having conversed at length until dawn, so he departed" | Structural |
| 12 | Bringing the boy | "they brought the boy alive" | "they brought the boy alive" | "they took him home alive" | "they brought the boy alive" | Interpretive expansion |
| 12 | Comfort level | "were comforted not moderately" | "were exceedingly comforted" | "the relief they felt was beyond words" | "were greatly comforted" | Word choice / interpretive |
| 13 | Taking Paul aboard | "from-there being-about to take-up Paul" | "intending from there to take Paul on board" | "where we planned to pick up Paul" | "intending from there to take Paul on board" | Word choice / structural |
| 13 | On foot | "being-about himself to go-on-foot" | "intending himself to go on foot" | "he intended to go there on foot" | "intending himself to go on foot" | Interpretive expansion |
| 14 | Boarding | "having taken him up" | "we took him on board" | "we took him aboard" | "we took him on board" | Word choice |
| 15 | Arrival at Chios | "on the following [day] we arrived opposite Chios" | "on the following day we arrived opposite Chios" | "we arrived the next day off Chios" | "on the following day we arrived opposite Chios" | Structural |
| 15 | Arrival at Miletus | "on the following [day] we came to Miletus" | "the day after we came to Miletus" | "the following day reached Miletus" | "the day after we came to Miletus" | Word choice / structural |
| 16 | Purpose clause | "so-that it might not happen to him to spend-time in Asia" | "so that he might not have to spend time in Asia" | "so he wouldn't lose time in the province of Asia" | "so that he might not have to spend time in Asia" | Structural / interpretive |
| 16 | Possibility clause | "if it were possible for him" | "if it were possible for him" | "if at all possible" | "if it were possible for him" | Word choice |
| 17 | ΕΚΚΛΗΣΙΑ rendering | "the assembly" | "the church" | "the church" | "the church" | Word choice (theological) |
| 18 | Self-reference emphasis | "You yourselves know" | "You yourselves know" | "You know" | "You yourselves know" | Word choice |
| 19 | Humility modifier | "with all humility and tears and trials" | "with all humility and with tears and with trials" | "with complete humility, through tears and through the ordeals" | "with all humility and with tears and with trials" | Word choice / structural |
| 19 | Trials description | "the [ones] having come-upon me by the plots of the Jews" | "that came upon me through the plots of the Jews" | "that came crashing down on me from Jewish conspiracies" | "that came upon me through the plots of the Jews" | Interpretive expansion |
| 20 | Holding back phrasing | "I held-back nothing of the [things] profitable" | "I did not shrink from declaring to you anything that was profitable" | "I never held anything back that would help you" | "I did not shrink from declaring to you anything that was profitable" | Structural / word choice |
| 20 | House to house | "publicly and from house-to-house" | "publicly and from house to house" | "publicly and went house to house" | "publicly and from house to house" | Structural |
| 21 | Main verb | "testifying" | "testifying" | "urging" | "testifying" | Word choice (theological) |
| 21 | Faith preposition | "faith toward our Lord Jesus Christ" | "faith in our Lord Jesus Christ" | "put their trust in our Lord Jesus" | "faith in our Lord Jesus Christ" | Word choice |
| 21 | Christological title | "Lord Jesus Christ" | "Lord Jesus Christ" | "Lord Jesus" (omits "Christ") | "Lord Jesus Christ" | Word choice (theological) |
| 22 | Spirit reference | "bound in the Spirit" | "bound in the Spirit" | "bound in my spirit" | "bound in the Spirit" | Interpretive (theological) |
| 22 | Unknown future | "the [things] in it [that will] meet me not knowing" | "not knowing what will happen to me there" | "not knowing what will happen to me there" | "not knowing what will happen to me there" | Structural |
| 23 | Spirit's action | "testifies to me" | "testifies to me" | "keeps warning me" | "testifies to me" | Word choice |
| 23 | Bonds/chains | "bonds and afflictions" | "bonds and afflictions" | "chains and suffering" | "bonds and afflictions" | Word choice |
| 24 | Life valuation | "I make of no account [my] life [as] precious to myself" | "I consider my life of no account as dear to myself" | "I don't consider my life worth anything to myself" | "I consider my life of no account as precious to myself" | Word choice / structural |
| 24 | Course/race | "my course" | "my course" | "the race" | "my course" | Word choice |
| 24 | Ministry/mission | "the ministry which I received" | "the ministry which I received" | "the mission the Lord Jesus gave me" | "the ministry which I received" | Word choice |
| 24 | Gospel phrasing | "to testify the good-news of the grace of God" | "to testify to the gospel of the grace of God" | "to be a witness to the good news of God's grace" | "to testify to the gospel of the grace of God" | Word choice / structural |
| 25 | Word order / face | "no-longer will you see my face, all of you among whom..." | "all of you, among whom...will see my face no more" | "none of you...will ever see my face again" | "all of you...will see my face no more" | Structural |
| 26 | Clean/innocent | "I [am] clean from the blood of all" | "I am innocent of the blood of all" | "I am clean of everyone's blood" | "I am innocent of the blood of all" | Word choice |
| 26 | Day emphasis | "on this very day" | "on this day" | "today" | "on this day" | Word choice |
| 27 | Counsel/plan | "all the counsel of God" | "the whole counsel of God" | "the whole plan of God" | "the whole counsel of God" | Word choice |
| 28 | Overseer placement | "placed you [as] overseers" | "has made you overseers" | "has placed in your care" | "has placed you as overseers" | Word choice / structural |
| 28 | ΕΚΚΛΗΣΙΑ rendering (v.28) | "the assembly of God" | "the church of God" | "the church of God" | "the church of God" | Word choice (theological) |
| 28 | Blood phrasing | "through the blood of his own [Son]" | "through his own blood" | "with his own blood" | "through the blood of his own" | **Theological** -- major interpretive difference. EMET supplies "[Son]"; LOGOS reads as God's own blood; PNEUMA changes preposition to "with." SOPHIA preserves Greek ambiguity |
| 28 | Obtained/purchased | "obtained" | "obtained" | "purchased" | "obtained" | Word choice |
| 29 | Wolf adjective | "grievous wolves" | "fierce wolves" | "savage wolves" | "fierce wolves" | Word choice |
| 30 | Distortion phrasing | "speaking distorted-things" | "speaking distorted things" | "twist the truth" | "speaking distorted things" | Interpretive expansion |
| 30 | Drawing away | "to draw-away the disciples after themselves" | "to draw away the disciples after themselves" | "to drag disciples away after them" | "to draw away the disciples after themselves" | Word choice |
| 31 | Watchfulness | "be-watchful" | "be watchful" | "stay alert" | "be watchful" | Word choice |
| 31 | Admonishing/warning | "admonishing each one" | "admonishing each one" | "warning each one of you" | "admonishing each one" | Word choice |
| 31 | Tears qualifier | "with tears" | "with tears" | "often with tears" | "with tears" | Interpretive expansion |
| 32 | Commend/hand over | "I commend you" | "I commend you" | "I hand you over" | "I commend you" | Word choice |
| 32 | Word/message | "the word of his grace" | "the word of his grace" | "the message of his grace" | "the word of his grace" | Word choice |
| 32 | Sanctified phrasing | "the [ones] having been sanctified" | "those who are sanctified" | "those who are set apart for him" | "those who have been sanctified" | Word choice / interpretive |
| 33 | Coveted/wanted | "did I covet" | "I coveted" | "I never wanted" | "I coveted" | Word choice |
| 33 | Word order | "Silver or gold or clothing of no-one did I covet" (inverted) | "I coveted no one's silver or gold or clothing" | "I never wanted anyone's silver or gold or clothing" | "I coveted no one's silver or gold or clothing" | Structural |
| 34 | Hands reference | "these hands ministered to my needs" | "these hands ministered to my own needs" | "these hands of mine provided for my own needs" | "these hands ministered to my own needs" | Word choice / interpretive |
| 34 | Companions | "to the [ones] being with me" | "to those who were with me" | "for the needs of those who were with me" | "to those who were with me" | Interpretive expansion |
| 35 | Laboring description | "thus laboring" | "by laboring in this way" | "by working hard like this" | "by laboring in this way" | Word choice |
| 35 | Necessity phrasing | "it is necessary to support" | "it is necessary to help" | "we must support" | "it is necessary to help" | Word choice / structural |
| 35 | Jesus saying | "'It is more blessed to give than to receive'" | "'It is more blessed to give than to receive'" | "'It is a greater blessing to give than to receive'" | "'It is more blessed to give than to receive'" | Word choice |
| 36 | Prayer sentence | "having knelt with all of them, he prayed" | "he knelt down and prayed with them all" | "he knelt down with all of them and prayed" | "he knelt down with them all and prayed" | Structural |
| 37 | Weeping description | "considerable weeping of all came-about" | "there was much weeping among them all" | "They all broke down weeping" | "there was much weeping among them all" | Structural / interpretive |
| 37 | Neck/kiss action | "having fallen-upon the neck of Paul, they were kissing him" | "they fell upon Paul's neck and kissed him" | "They threw their arms around Paul's neck and kissed him" | "they fell upon Paul's neck and kissed him" | Word choice / interpretive |
| 38 | Grief word | "grieving most-of-all" | "grieving especially" | "heartbroken most of all" | "grieving most of all" | Word choice |
| 38 | Face phrasing | "they are-about no-longer to behold his face" | "they would see his face no more" | "they would never see his face again" | "they would see his face no more" | Structural |
| 38 | Escort to ship | "they were escorting him to the ship" | "they accompanied him to the ship" | "they walked him to the ship" | "they accompanied him to the ship" | Word choice |

---

## Key Theological Disagreements

### 1. Verse 28 -- "Church of God" (textual variant)
All three translators correctly followed Sinaiticus reading ΕΚΚΛΗΣΙΑΝ ΤΟΥ ΘΕΟΥ ("church of God") rather than the variant "church of the Lord" (ΤΟΥ ΚΥΡΙΟΥ). EMET and LOGOS rendered ΕΚΚΛΗΣΙΑ differently: EMET used "assembly" while LOGOS and PNEUMA used "church." SOPHIA follows "church" as the standard translation in ecclesiological context.

### 2. Verse 28 -- "Through the blood of his own" (Christological ambiguity)
This is the most significant theological disagreement in the chapter. The Greek ΔΙΑ ΤΟΥ ΑΙΜΑΤΟΣ ΤΟΥ ΙΔΙΟΥ admits three readings:
- **EMET:** "through the blood of his own [Son]" -- supplies "[Son]" to resolve ambiguity, treating ΙΔΙΟΥ as substantive
- **LOGOS:** "through his own blood" -- reads ΙΔΙΟΥ as simple possessive, creating the striking claim that God obtained the church through his own blood
- **PNEUMA:** "with his own blood" -- same as LOGOS but changes preposition from "through" to "with"
- **SOPHIA:** "through the blood of his own" -- preserves the precise Greek construction and its ambiguity without editorial resolution

### 3. Verse 22 -- "Bound in the Spirit" vs "bound in my spirit"
- **EMET/LOGOS:** "bound in the Spirit" (capital S, referring to Holy Spirit)
- **PNEUMA:** "bound in my spirit" (lowercase, Paul's inner compulsion)
- **SOPHIA:** "bound in the Spirit" -- The Greek ΤΩ ΠΝΕΥΜΑΤΙ is ambiguous, but the context of v.23 (Holy Spirit testifying) favors the divine referent. Capital S signals this while the reader may hold both meanings.

### 4. Verse 21 -- "Lord Jesus Christ" vs "Lord Jesus"
- **EMET/LOGOS:** "Lord Jesus Christ" (full Sinaiticus reading ΚΥΡΙΟΝ ΙΗΣΟΥΝ ΧΡΙΣΤΟΝ)
- **PNEUMA:** "Lord Jesus" (omits "Christ")
- **SOPHIA:** "Lord Jesus Christ" -- follows the Sinaiticus text as read by two of three translators

### 5. Verse 25 -- "The kingdom" (without "of God")
All three translators correctly preserved the shorter Sinaiticus reading "the kingdom" (ΤΗΝ ΒΑΣΙΛΕΙΑΝ) without adding "of God." No disagreement here -- noted for textual significance.

### 6. Verse 35 -- Agraphon of Jesus
Minor rendering difference: EMET/LOGOS: "It is more blessed to give than to receive"; PNEUMA: "It is a greater blessing to give than to receive." SOPHIA follows EMET/LOGOS majority. The saying itself is the most famous unrecorded word of Jesus, not found in any canonical Gospel.

### 7. Verse 9 -- Eutychus: Dead or Apparently Dead?
All three translators rendered ΗΡΘΗ ΝΕΚΡΟΣ as genuinely dead ("was taken up dead" / "was picked up dead"). No disagreement on this point -- Luke states it plainly. The theological question of miracle vs. resuscitation is left to the reader.

### 8. Verse 17 -- "Assembly" vs "Church"
EMET consistently renders ΕΚΚΛΗΣΙΑ as "assembly" (vv. 17, 28) while LOGOS and PNEUMA use "church." SOPHIA follows LOGOS/PNEUMA. The term "assembly" is etymologically accurate but "church" is the established English convention for ΕΚΚΛΗΣΙΑ in the New Testament ecclesiological sense.
